intro: Stisseria geminata, also known as Cynanchum geminatum, is a perennial climbing plant native to South Africa, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, with health benefits reported to include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Stisseria geminata include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stisseria geminata is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also incorporated into various herbal teas and infusions to leverage its health benefits.
